notes: Reproduced: SKCanvasView in Blazor WASM shows severe performance degradation when drawing a scaled SKImage. At DPI=2 (1596x1596 canvas), 1:1 drawing takes ~1.2ms/frame while any scaled drawing takes ~42ms/frame — a 35x slowdown. This matches the reporter's observation of 120fps dropping to sub-20fps. Cross-platform verification confirms this is dramatically worse on WASM: native macOS ARM64 takes 1.6ms for scaled draws (26-27x faster than WASM for the same operation). The scaling penalty exists on native too (0.38ms vs 1.57ms, ~4x), but WASM amplifies it by an additional 26x factor.

versionResults.notes: Reporter's version. Scaled image draw ~42ms/frame (24 FPS) vs 1:1 draw ~1.2ms/frame (833 FPS) on 1596x1596 canvas in Chrome.
Cross-platform: native macOS console test shows scaled draw at 1.57ms/frame — usable performance. WASM is 26-27x slower for the same operation.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.0 and WasmBuildNative enabled
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net9.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.0

reproductionSteps.description: Write reproduction page with SKCanvasView in EnableRenderLoop mode, creating a 786x1090 test image and drawing it in multiple scale modes (1:1, scaled-2x, canvas.Scale(2)), measuring per-frame render time via Stopwatch
Build Blazor WASM project with native compilation

Serve app and verify in Chrome via Playwright MCP. Default mode (scaled-2x) shows ~42ms/frame (24 FPS) on 1596x1596 canvas. Browser console confirms: PERF: mode=scaled-2x frameMs=42.0 avgMs=42.0 fps=24 canvas=1596x1596
reproductionSteps.output: PERF: mode=scaled-2x frameMs=42.0 avgMs=42.0 fps=24 canvas=1596x1596

reproductionSteps.description: Switch to 1:1 mode (draw image at exact 786x1090 dimensions). Console shows ~1.2ms/frame (833 FPS) — 35x faster than scaled mode. This confirms the reporter's observation that 1:1 drawing is fast while any scaling tanks performance.
reproductionSteps.output: PERF: mode=1to1 frameMs=1.2 avgMs=1.2 fps=833 canvas=1596x1596

reproductionSteps.description: Switch to canvas-scale-2x mode (canvas.Scale(2) then draw at original 786x1090 rect — reporter's exact scenario). Shows ~42.9ms/frame (23 FPS), matching reporter's observation of sub-20fps with canvas.Scale(2).
reproductionSteps.output: PERF: mode=canvas-scale-2x frameMs=42.9 avgMs=42.9 fps=23 canvas=1596x1596

reproductionSteps.description: Cross-platform verification: run same DrawImage operations natively on macOS ARM64 console app. Results: 1:1=0.38ms, scaled-2x=1.57ms, canvas-scale-2x=1.63ms. Native is 26-27x faster for scaled draws, confirming the performance issue is WASM-specific amplification.
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet run -c Release (console app with same draw operations)
reproductionSteps.output:
1:1 draw: 0.38ms avg
scaled-2x draw: 1.57ms avg
canvas-scale-2x draw: 1.63ms avg
Platform: Darwin Arm64

errorMessages.primaryError: SKCanvasView scaled image drawing takes ~42ms/frame (24 FPS) in Blazor WASM vs ~1.6ms/frame natively — 26-27x performance degradation making scaled rendering unusable

actionability.reason: Reproduced with clear quantitative evidence. Scaled image drawing in Blazor WASM SKCanvasView is 26-27x slower than native, making it unusable at ~24 FPS. The 35x gap between 1:1 and scaled within WASM suggests the bottleneck is in Skia's raster resampling path running in WASM. Needs investigation into whether WASM SIMD optimizations or alternative rendering strategies can close this gap.

actions.description: Post reproduction findings with measured data
actions.comment:
We reproduced this with a minimal Blazor WASM app on SkiaSharp 3.119.0. Drawing a 786x1090 SKImage into a 1596x1596 canvas (DPI=2):

| Mode | WASM (Chrome) | Native (macOS ARM64) | WASM slowdown |
|------|--------------|---------------------|---------------|
| 1:1 | 1.2ms (833 FPS) | 0.38ms | 3x |
| scaled-2x | 42ms (24 FPS) | 1.57ms | 27x |
| canvas.Scale(2) | 43ms (23 FPS) | 1.63ms | 26x |

The scaling penalty exists on native too (~4x), but WASM amplifies it by an additional 26x factor. We're investigating optimization opportunities in the WASM raster path.

proposedResponse.summary: Reproduced: SKCanvasView scaled image drawing is 26-27x slower on WASM than native, making it unusable for real-time rendering.
proposedResponse.body:
We reproduced this with a minimal Blazor WASM app on SkiaSharp 3.119.0. Drawing a 786x1090 SKImage into a 1596x1596 canvas (DPI=2):

| Mode | WASM (Chrome) | Native (macOS ARM64) | WASM slowdown |
|------|--------------|---------------------|---------------|
| 1:1 | 1.2ms (833 FPS) | 0.38ms | 3x |
| scaled-2x | 42ms (24 FPS) | 1.57ms | 27x |
| canvas.Scale(2) | 43ms (23 FPS) | 1.63ms | 26x |

The scaling penalty exists on native too (~4x slower than 1:1), but WASM amplifies it by an additional 26x factor, matching your observation of 120fps dropping to sub-20fps.

We're investigating optimization opportunities in the WASM raster path.
